title: [HIGH] Preserve MoeError JSON-RPC codes in MCP adapter
description: Deep sweep finding: packages/moe-daemon/src/server/McpAdapter.ts catches tool errors and always returns JSON-RPC code -32000, discarding MoeError.code values such as INVALID_INPUT (-32602) and NOT_ALLOWED (-32003). Additionally util/errors.ts formats messages with enum reverse lookup despite duplicate numeric enum values, so messages can show the wrong category name (e.g. NOT_FOUND may render as STEP_NOT_FOUND).
definitionOfDone: McpAdapter detects MoeError and returns its actual code/message without exposing stack traces.
Non-MoeError failures still return safe internal error responses.
MoeError message formatting uses an explicit code-name field or unique names so duplicate numeric values do not produce misleading prefixes.
Regression tests cover invalid input, not allowed, and generic thrown Error cases.
packages/moe-daemon npm test and build pass.

implementationPlan.description: Update MoeError formatting to avoid duplicate enum reverse-lookup names while preserving numeric code values and safe client messages.
implementationPlan.note: Updated MoeError formatting to stop using duplicate numeric enum reverse lookups. MoeError now stores an explicit codeName; factories and direct callers pass stable names (MISSING_REQUIRED, NOT_FOUND, NOT_ALLOWED, etc.), while numeric JSON-RPC code values remain unchanged. Added a red/green formatting regression in enforcement.test: before fix missingRequired rendered as [INVALID_ID]; after fix explicit-name test passes. Verification: `npx vitest run src/util/enforcement.test.ts -t "explicit names"` failed before fix then passed; full `npx vitest run src/util/enforcement.test.ts` passed 14/14.

implementationPlan.description: Update McpAdapter tool error handling to detect MoeError, return the actual JSON-RPC code and safe data, and keep generic error handling at -32000.
implementationPlan.note: Updated McpAdapter tool error handling to detect MoeError and return its actual JSON-RPC code/message with compact safe data `{ tool, codeName }`; generic thrown Error remains -32000 with tool-only data and no stack exposure. Added red/green adapter regressions for invalid input, not allowed, and not found: before fix they failed because all returned -32000; after fix the MoeError tests pass. Verification: `npx vitest run src/server/McpAdapter.test.ts -t "preserves MoeError"` failed before adapter change then passed; full `npx vitest run src/server/McpAdapter.test.ts` passed 16/16.

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests for invalid input (-32602), not allowed (-32003), not found/invalid state as appropriate, and generic Error fallback; run daemon test/build validation.
implementationPlan.note: Completed regression and validation. Added tests for MoeError formatting with duplicate numeric codes, McpAdapter invalid-input (-32602), not-allowed (-32003), not-found (-32001), invalid-state (-32002), and preserved generic Error fallback (-32000 existing test). Red/green evidence: explicit-name test failed before formatting fix (`[INVALID_ID]` instead of `[MISSING_REQUIRED]`); adapter MoeError tests failed before adapter fix because responses were -32000; both passed after fixes. Adversarial review: client error.data contains only `{ tool, codeName }`, no args/context/stack; generic errors still -32000; existing STEP_NOT_FOUND behavior preserved via specific notFound code names. Verification: targeted tests passed; final daemon `npm test` passed 25 files / 433 tests; `npm run build` passed; `git diff --check` on affected files exited 0 (CRLF warnings only).

comments.author: architect-2992
comments.content: Token-budget check: when preserving MoeError codes, keep JSON-RPC error payloads safe and compact. Do not include stacks, full request args, or large context blobs in error.data.

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Preferred: import MoeError in McpAdapter and use toClientObject/code; replace reverse enum formatting with explicit codeName argument/map. Rejected: parsing code names out of message strings.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: Many tools throw MoeError with specific JSON-RPC-compatible codes, but McpAdapter currently collapses all tool errors to -32000. MoeError enum has duplicate numeric values, so reverse enum name lookup is misleading.
planningNotes.risks: Do not expose stack traces or sensitive context. Preserve generic -32000 behavior for unexpected errors while improving structured error handling for known MoeError cases.
